notes: Assertion 'spawns subagent' fails in both configurations for eval 1 because the Agent tool is not available inside eval executor subagents. This is an eval harness constraint, not a skill defect — the SKILL.md correctly instructs the agent to spawn a subagent.
Eval 3 (staged-no-changes-exit) is non-discriminating: both configurations score 100%. The empty-staged-changes warning is obvious enough that the baseline handles it correctly without the skill.

notes: Non-discriminating eval — baseline handles this correctly without the skill. Eval establishes baseline behavior only.
Eval 2 is the primary discriminating eval (+0.40 delta): spec mode detection and structured reviewer delegation differentiate with_skill from without_skill.

Eval 1 is zero-delta (0.80/0.80) due to an eval harness constraint — Agent tool unavailable in eval executor context masks the subagent-spawning differentiator. This is not non-discriminating in the CLAUDE.md sense (not 100%/100%).
Eval 3 is non-discriminating (both 100%): empty-staged-changes handling is obvious enough for the baseline to handle correctly.
